_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:21Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./komodo-setup.sh`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	# Komodo-compatible setup script for mailcow
5	# This script initializes mailcow.conf from the template with generated secrets.
6	# Run once on initial deployment. Subsequent deploys (via Komodo webhook) only
7	# need `docker compose up -d` which Komodo handles automatically.
8	
9	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}")" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd)"
10	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
11	
12	echo "=== mailcow Komodo Setup for mail.lgucalapan.ph ==="
13	
14	# Check if mailcow.conf already exists
15	if [ -f mailcow.conf ]; then
16	  echo "mailcow.conf already exists."
17	  read -r -p "Overwrite? This will back up the current config. [y/N] " response
18	  case $response in
19	    [yY][eE][sS]|[yY])
20	      cp mailcow.conf "mailcow.conf.bak.$(date +%s)"
21	      echo "Backed up existing config."
22	      ;;
23	    *)
24	      echo "Keeping existing config. Exiting."
25	      exit 0
26	      ;;
27	  esac
28	fi
29	
30	# Generate random secrets
31	gen_secret() {
32	  LC_ALL=C </dev/urandom tr -dc A-Za-z0-9 2>/dev/null | head -c "${1:-28}"
33	}
34	
35	DBPASS=$(gen_secret 28)
36	DBROOT=$(gen_secret 28)
37	REDISPASS=$(gen_secret 28)
38	SOGO_KEY=$(gen_secret 16)
39	
40	echo "Generating mailcow.conf from template..."
41	
42	sed \
43	  -e "s/__DBPASS__/${DBPASS}/" \
44	  -e "s/__DBROOT__/${DBROOT}/" \
45	  -e "s/__REDISPASS__/${REDISPASS}/" \
46	  -e "s/__SOGO_KEY__/${SOGO_KEY}/" \
47	  mailcow.conf.template > mailcow.conf
48	
49	chmod 600 mailcow.conf
50	
51	# Create .env symlink if not present
52	if [ ! -L .env ]; then
53	  ln -sf mailcow.conf .env
54	  echo "Created .env -> mailcow.conf symlink."
55	fi
56	
57	# Create required directories
58	mkdir -p data/assets/ssl
59	
60	# Generate snake-oil certificate if no cert exists
61	if [ ! -f data/assets/ssl/cert.pem ]; then
62	  echo "Generating snake-oil SSL certificate..."
63	  openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 \
64	    -keyout data/assets/ssl/key.pem \
65	    -out data/assets/ssl/cert.pem \
66	    -days 365 \
67	    -subj "/C=PH/ST=Calapan/L=Calapan/O=lgucalapan/OU=mail/CN=mail.lgucalapan.ph" \
68	    -sha256 -nodes 2>/dev/null
69	  echo "Snake-oil certificate generated. Let's Encrypt will replace it automatically."
70	fi
71	
72	# Create rspamd password placeholder if missing
73	[ ! -f ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/worker-controller-password.inc ] && \
74	  echo '# Placeholder' > ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/worker-controller-password.inc
75	
76	# Set app_info
77	mailcow_git_version=$(git describe --tags "$(git rev-list --tags --max-count=1)" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse --short HEAD)
78	mailcow_git_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
79	mailcow_git_commit_date=$(git log -1 --format=%ci 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
80	git_branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "master")
81	
82	cat > data/web/inc/app_info.inc.php << APPEOF
83	<?php
84	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_VERSION="${mailcow_git_version}";
85	  \$MAILCOW_LAST_GIT_VERSION="";
86	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_OWNER="bonitobonita24";
87	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_REPO="mail_lgucalapan_ph";
88	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_URL="https://github.com/bonitobonita24/mail_lgucalapan_ph";
89	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_COMMIT="${mailcow_git_commit}";
90	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_COMMIT_DATE="${mailcow_git_commit_date}";
91	  \$MAILCOW_BRANCH="${git_branch}";
92	  \$MAILCOW_UPDATEDAT=$(date +%s);
93	?>
94	APPEOF
95	
96	echo ""
97	echo "=== Setup Complete ==="
98	echo ""
99	echo "  Hostname: mail.lgucalapan.ph"
100	echo "  Timezone: Asia/Manila"
101	echo "  Config:   mailcow.conf (chmod 600)"
102	echo ""
103	echo "Next steps:"
104	echo "  1. docker compose pull"
105	echo "  2. docker compose up -d"
106	echo "  3. Access https://mail.lgucalapan.ph"
107	echo "  4. Login: admin / moohoo (change immediately!)"
108	echo ""
109	echo "DNS records required:"
110	echo "  A/AAAA  mail.lgucalapan.ph          -> your server IP"
111	echo "  MX      lgucalapan.ph               -> mail.lgucalapan.ph (priority 10)"
112	echo "  TXT     lgucalapan.ph               -> v=spf1 mx a -all"
113	echo "  TXT     _dmarc.lgucalapan.ph        -> v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:admin@lgucalapan.ph"
114	echo "  TXT     dkim._domainkey.lgucalapan.ph -> (get from mailcow admin UI after first start)"
115	echo "  CNAME   autodiscover.lgucalapan.ph  -> mail.lgucalapan.ph"
116	echo "  CNAME   autoconfig.lgucalapan.ph    -> mail.lgucalapan.ph"
117	echo "  SRV     _autodiscover._tcp.lgucalapan.ph -> 0 1 443 mail.lgucalapan.ph"
118
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:29Z)**_

The problem is `set -euo pipefail` combined with the `gen_secret()` function on line 32. When `head -c 28` gets enough bytes it closes the pipe, causing `tr` to get a `SIGPIPE` / non-zero exit — and `pipefail` treats that as a failure, silently killing the script.

The trace stops after the echo on line 12 because `mailcow.conf` doesn't exist (skips the `if` block), then hits line 35 `DBPASS=$(gen_secret 28)` which fails due to the broken pipe.

Here's the fix:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:31Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/komodo-setup.sh has been updated successfully.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:36Z)**_

Also need to fix line 73 — the rspamd directory likely doesn't exist yet, which would fail silently or error out:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:38Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/komodo-setup.sh has been updated successfully.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:41Z)**_

And line 82 — the `data/web/inc/` directory also won't exist on a fresh deploy:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:43Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/komodo-setup.sh has been updated successfully.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:50Z)**_

**Summary of fixes:**

1. **`gen_secret()` — the actual crash** — `tr | head` triggers SIGPIPE when `head` closes early. With `pipefail`, that's a fatal error. Added `|| true` to suppress it.
2. **Missing rspamd directory** — added `mkdir -p` before writing the placeholder file.
3. **Missing `data/web/inc/` directory** — added `mkdir -p` before writing `app_info.inc.php`.

Push this and re-run the script on your server. It should get past the secret generation now.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:32:25Z)**_

Setup completed successfully. The `|| true` fix on `gen_secret()` was the key — SIGPIPE from `tr | head` was killing the script under `pipefail`.

You're ready for `docker compose pull && docker compose up -d` now. Make sure your DNS records are in place first if you haven't set those up yet.

---

_**User (2026-04-12 16:33:22Z)**_

but should i really run docker compose pull and docker compose up -d from the terminal and not from Komodo?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:33:28Z)**_



Good point — you should do it from Komodo since that's your deployment manager. Running it manually from the terminal would bypass Komodo's tracking of container state.

Just trigger the deploy/rebuild from the Komodo UI for the `mail-lgucalapan-ph` stack. It will handle the pull and `up -d` for you.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:40:53Z)**_



Port 80 is already in use on the server — likely by Komodo's own reverse proxy or another web server (nginx/caddy/traefik). The mailcow nginx container can't bind to it directly.

Since you're running behind Komodo, you probably want to put mailcow's nginx behind Komodo's reverse proxy instead of having it bind ports 80/443 directly. Let me check your compose file to see the port mappings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:41:02Z)**_

There it is — lines 454-455. The nginx container binds to `0.0.0.0:80` and `0.0.0.0:443` by default.

What's already using port 80 on the server? Is it Komodo's reverse proxy (like a Caddy or Traefin instance)? If so, you have two options:

1. **Remap mailcow's HTTP/HTTPS to different ports** and proxy through Komodo's reverse proxy — set `HTTP_PORT=8080` and `HTTPS_PORT=8443` in `mailcow.conf`
2. **Bind to localhost only** — set `HTTP_BIND=127.0.0.1` and `HTTPS_BIND=127.0.0.1` in `mailcow.conf`, then point your existing reverse proxy to `127.0.0.1:80`

Which setup are you using for Komodo — is there a reverse proxy (Caddy, Traefik, nginx) in front handling `kmd.lgucalapan.ph`?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:43:49Z)**_

```
ss -tlnp | grep ':80'
```

or

```
docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Ports}}' | grep 80
```

That will tell you what process/container is currently holding port 80. Paste the output and I can tell you what you're working with.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:44:23Z)**_



Traefik is your reverse proxy on port 80. So the approach is:

1. **Stop mailcow's nginx from binding 80/443 directly** — let Traefik handle incoming traffic
2. **Add Traefik labels** to the mailcow nginx container so Traefik routes `mail.lgucalapan.ph` to it

Let me check your mailcow.conf template and the Traefik network setup.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:45:15Z)**_

I'm running commands locally, not on your server. But from the `ss` output, Traefik is running directly on the host (pid, not inside a Docker container).

Here's what you need to do:

**1. Update `mailcow.conf` to bind to localhost on different ports:**

On the server, edit `/etc/komodo/stacks/mail-lgucalapan-ph/mailcow.conf` and set:

```
HTTP_PORT=8080
HTTP_BIND=127.0.0.1
HTTPS_PORT=8443
HTTPS_BIND=127.0.0.1
```

**2. Configure Traefik to route `mail.lgucalapan.ph` to mailcow:**

You'll need a Traefik file provider config (or however your Traefik is configured) with something like:

```yaml
http:
  routers:
    mailcow:
      rule: "Host(`mail.lgucalapan.ph`)"
      entryPoints:
        - websecure
      service: mailcow
      tls:
        certResolver: letsencrypt  # or whatever your resolver is named
  services:
    mailcow:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:8080"
```

**3. Redeploy the stack from Komodo.**

Also — mailcow needs ports **25, 143, 465, 587, 993, 995** for actual mail traffic (SMTP/IMAP/POP). Those are not HTTP, so Traefik won't proxy them. Check that nothing else is using those:

```
ss -tlnp | grep -E ':(25|143|465|587|993|995) '
```

If those ports are free, they'll bind fine as-is. Only the HTTP/HTTPS ports conflict with Traefik.

Want me to update the `mailcow.conf.template` and `komodo-setup.sh` with these bind settings, or do you want to handle it on the server only?

---

api:
  dashboard: false

entryPoints:
  web:
    address: ":80"
    http:
      redirections:
        entryPoint:
          to: websecure
          scheme: https
  websecure:
    address: ":443"

certificatesResolvers:

    acme:
      email: admin@lgucalapan.ph
      storage: /acme/acme.json
      httpChallenge:
        entryPoint: web

providers:

    watch: true
http:

    komodo:

      service: komodo

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:50:54Z)**_



Add the mailcow router and service to `/etc/traefik/dynamic.yml`:

```yaml
http:
  routers:
    komodo:
      rule: "Host(`kmd.lgucalapan.ph`)"
      entryPoints:
        - websecure
      service: komodo
      tls:
        certResolver: letsencrypt

    mailcow:
      rule: "Host(`mail.lgucalapan.ph`)"
      entryPoints:
        - websecure
      service: mailcow
      tls:
        certResolver: letsencrypt

  services:
    komodo:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://host.docker.internal:9120"

    mailcow:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:8080"
```

Since Traefik has `watch: true` on that file, it'll pick up the change automatically — no restart needed. After that, redeploy the mailcow stack from Komodo and `https://mail.lgucalapan.ph` should come up.
